skill.name: seo-fundamentals
skill.description: Review HTML, React, Next.js, and Remix pages for core SEO basics, semantic structure, metadata, alt text, and social tags.
skill.summary: Guides SEO reviews for page metadata, headings, semantic HTML, alt text, and social sharing tags.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and hardcoded URL patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found the command and cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown, HTML, TSX, and SEO checklist examples. The skill is safe to publish with a low network note because it suggests optional external documentation and repository lookup through MCP tools.

low_findings.title: Static Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown checklist items, fenced HTML examples, fenced TSX examples, or plain text prompts. No executable Ruby, shell command, user input interpolation, or command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown and HTML examples, not executable code. There is no shell API, Ruby code, or instruction to run local commands.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography Alerts Are SEO Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain SEO terms, metadata examples, HTML tags, title guidance, and descriptive text. No cryptographic API, hashing call, cipher selection, or credential handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only SEO checklist language and markup examples at the cited lines. There is no cryptographic behavior to evaluate or block.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Open Graph URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are example values for og:image and og:url in an Open Graph template. They do not send data, load remote content, or instruct credential transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use yoursite.com placeholders inside a fenced HTML template. They are documentation examples, not active network calls.

low_findings.title: Optional External Documentation and Repository Lookup
low_findings.description: The skill suggests using Context7 to fetch framework documentation and Octocode to search real examples. This is legitimate for SEO review work, but it can involve network access when those MCP tools are used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly names fetch and search actions through external MCP tools. No evidence indicates secret access, credential collection, or data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Improve SEO Reviews for Web Pages
content.value_statement: Teams often miss basic SEO metadata and semantic structure during code review. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for practical page optimization.
actual_capabilities: Checks title tags, meta descriptions, H1 usage, heading hierarchy, semantic HTML, and image alt text.
Reviews Open Graph tags, Twitter Card tags, canonical URLs, and structured data needs for marketing pages.
Provides guidance for Next.js App Router, Next.js Pages Router, plain HTML, and React metadata patterns.
Uses Socratic questions to guide code review discussions about discoverability and accessibility.
Flags common SEO anti-patterns such as multiple H1 tags, missing alt text, div-only layouts, and generic titles.
Connects SEO review with Core Web Vitals awareness for LCP, FID, and CLS.
limitations: It does not run crawlers, Lighthouse audits, live SERP checks, or analytics reports.
It does not guarantee higher search rankings or replace a full technical SEO strategy.
It focuses on page-level fundamentals, not advanced schema strategy or backlink analysis.
External documentation and repository examples require available MCP tools when those steps are used.

use_cases.title: Review a Marketing Page Before Launch
use_cases.description: Check page metadata, social preview tags, headings, and image alt text before publishing a campaign page.

use_cases.title: Coach Junior Developers on SEO Basics
use_cases.description: Use review questions to teach why semantic HTML, title tags, and descriptions affect search visibility.

use_cases.title: Audit Framework Metadata Patterns
use_cases.description: Compare Next.js App Router, Pages Router, React, or plain HTML implementations against expected SEO patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page SEO Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for SEO fundamentals. Check the title, meta description, H1, heading order, semantic HTML, and image alt text.

prompt_templates.title: Next.js Metadata Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a framework page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Next.js page for metadata quality. Check title, description, Open Graph tags, canonical URL needs, and social sharing gaps.

prompt_templates.title: SEO Code Review Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a code reviewer. Identify SEO issues in this page and write concise review comments with suggested fixes and priority levels.

prompt_templates.title: Launch Readiness SEO Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for launch readiness. Evaluate semantic structure, metadata coverage, social preview tags, structured data opportunities, and Core Web Vitals concerns.

output_examples.input: A React landing page with three H1 tags, missing meta description, and decorative images without useful alt text.
output: High priority: Use one H1 that describes the main page topic.
High priority: Add a concise meta description for search result snippets.
Medium priority: Add meaningful alt text for informative images and empty alt text for decorative images.

output_examples.input: A Next.js App Router page with a title and description but no social preview metadata.
output: Add Open Graph title, description, image, URL, and type fields for social sharing.
Confirm whether the page needs a canonical URL to avoid duplicate content issues.

output_examples.input: A plain HTML page that uses only div elements for header, navigation, content, and footer areas.
output: Replace structural div elements with header, nav, main, article, and footer where appropriate.
Keep heading levels sequential so users and search engines can understand the page outline.

best_practices: Review SEO fundamentals during normal code review, before the page reaches production.
Treat metadata, headings, semantic HTML, and alt text as part of user experience quality.
Use framework documentation when checking metadata APIs or router-specific behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not add multiple H1 tags just because sections need large visual headings.
Do not use generic titles such as Home or Page for indexable content.
Do not replace semantic structure with div-only layouts when HTML landmarks fit the content.

faq.question: Does this skill perform live SEO scans?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and markup for SEO fundamentals. It does not crawl websites or run Lighthouse.

faq.question: Can it review Next.js pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Next.js App Router metadata exports and Pages Router Head usage.

faq.question: Does it cover social sharing metadata?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks Open Graph and Twitter Card basics for pages that need rich social previews.

faq.question: Is this only for marketing pages?
faq.answer: No. It applies to any page that should be indexed, with extra checks for marketing pages.

faq.question: Does it replace an SEO specialist?
faq.answer: No. It helps with implementation fundamentals, but it does not replace strategy, analytics, or content planning.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention network use?
faq.answer: The skill suggests optional MCP documentation fetches and repository searches. These actions may use network access when available.
